title: anthriscus sylvestris subsp. mollis
intro: Anthriscus sylvestris subsp. mollis, also known as wild chervil or field chervil, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Anthriscus sylvestris subsp. mollis include its ability to aid digestion, reduce menstrual cramps, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in cooking as a garnish or added to salads for its mild anise-like flavor.
